person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
Armoirie de la famille de Baux :
Bulle Avers par Bertrand de Baux de 1200 à 1299
Dans le champ, un cornet et un lambel de six pendants.
Légende théorique
+ S BTRANDI DE .. VCIO DNI CAMARETI
Légende transcrite
Sigillum Bertrandi de Baucio, domini Camareti.
Bulle revers : 
Une croix grecque occupant tout le champ.

Bertrand des Baux (vers 1137 – 1181/1183), seigneur des Baux, est un prince d’Orange, le premier de la maison des Baux.
Il est le fils cadet de Raymond des Baux († 1150), seigneur des Baux, et d'Étiennette de Provence († vers 1163). Étiennette est la fille de Gilbert Ier de Gévaudan, vicomte de Millau et de Lodève, puis comte de Gévaudan et, par mariage, comte de Provence, et de Gerberge de Provence, comtesse de Provence de 1093 à 1111.

Étiennette est sœur de Douce de Gévaudan, épouse de Raimond-Bérenger III, comte de Barcelone. Quand cette dernière meurt, la maison des Baux affirme sa prétention sur le comté de Provence, débutant ainsi les Guerres baussenques.
Entre 1145 et 1162, les seigneurs des Baux entrent en guerre contre la maison de Barcelone dont ils contestent les droits sur la Provence; appuyés un moment par l'Empereur Romain Germanique, ils devront finalement se soumettre après avoir subi un siège aux Baux mêmes.
Ils portent des titres divers: les uns deviennent Princes d'ORANGE, d'autres Vicomtes de Marseille, d'autre encore ayant suivi en Italie du Sud l'expédition des princes capétiens d'ANJOU, sont faits comtes d'Avellino puis ducs d' Andria.
 En 1178 les princes des Baux sont autorisés par Barberousse, empereur d'Allemegne, à battre monaies d'or. Les sols d'or Baussenques émis depuis cette date jusqu'en 1620 portent l'étoile à seize rays.
En 1173 Bertrand des Baux prince d'Orange se marie avec Thiburge de Montpellier. Ils eurent :
1) Thiburge des Baux (1174-1215) mariée à Lambert Ier d'Adhémar de Monteil ;
2) Hugues V (ou Ugues) des Baux (1175-1239) marié à Baralle de Marseille ;
3) Bertrand des Baux (1177-1201) marié à Estefania ;
4) Guillem des Baux (1180-1218) marié à Ermengarz de Mévouillon (d'où Tiburge des Baux (unie avec Giraud III Ami Conétable à vérifier), et Raimon I des Baux), puis marié en secondes noces à Alix Eloy ( d'où Guillem II le troubadour et Tiburge des Baux (uni à Giraud V Amic de Sabran)

Meurt en 1181 château de Baux de Provence.

Sources :
- DOUET D'ARCQ L., Archives de l'Empire. Inventaires et documents publiés par ordre de l'Empereur. Collection de sceaux, Paris, 1865 ,  n° 1330 ;
- arbre Pierfit, Laurent de la Barre ;
relevé JPR Teyssier Chalancon Mangas

zh.lifeSummary: 當Bertrand Ier de Baux d'Orange於1137年出生在法國布-度-o萊斯 包-德-普洛福斯時，他的父親Raymond Ier des Baux55歲，母親Étiennette de Gévaudan-Provence39歲。他和Thiburge de Montpellier至少有3個兒子和3個女兒。他去世於1181年4月5日在法國普洛福-阿爾培-o特 達阿爾布-度-o拉 洛可-達藍泰隆享年44歲，埋葬在法國布-度-o拉 洛可-達藍泰隆。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Baux
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Indian (mainly West Bengal) and Bangladeshi: from Bengali baul, Sanskrit vatula ‘madman’. Bearers of this name traditionally lived a lifestyle characterized by isolation from society, typically singing of social inequalities and injustice. They subsisted mainly on alms, considering themselves to be outside any organized religion, representing instead a polymorphic spiritual tradition.
German: relationship name from a term denoting a relative or close friend, from Middle High German buole, Middle Low German bole ‘relative, lover, close friend’.
German: possibly also from a rare variant of the personal name   Paul  .

variants.name: Paul
meanings: English, French, German, Dutch, Swedish, West Indian (mainly Haiti, also e.g. Saint Lucia), and African (mainly Nigeria and Tanzania): from the personal name Paul (from Latin Paulus ‘small’), which has always been popular in Christendom. It was the name adopted by the Pharisee Saul of Tarsus after his conversion to Christianity on the road to Damascus in about AD 34. He was a most energetic missionary to the Gentiles in the Roman Empire, and played a very significant role in establishing Christianity as a major world religion. The name was borne also by numerous other early Christian saints. It is also occasionally borne by Jews; the reasons for this are not clear. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed many cognates from other languages and their patronymics and other derivatives, e.g. Greek Pavlis , Slovenian Pavel and Pavlič (see Pavlic ), Polish Paweł (see Pawel ) and Pawlicki , Assyrian/Chaldean Polous and Polus . In France, this surname is most common in Brittany (see 2 below).
Breton (mainly Finistère): from a Frenchified form of the personal name Paol, Breton form of Paul .
Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Phóil ‘son of Paul’. Compare McFall .

nameMeanings.name: d'Orange
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: French: habitational name from one of the many places called Orange in France, perhaps most prominently Orange in Vaucluse, from which the noble House of Orange took its name. It is also found in England, as a surname of Huguenot origin.

English: usually a variant of Orridge, either from the Middle English personal name Ordrich, Orrich (Old English Ordrīc, from ord ‘point, spear’ + rīc ‘power, realm’), with post-medieval voicing of the final consonant to give Orridge, or from a de-aspirated form of Horridge and Horwich (both habitational names from any of the places called Horwich in Lancashire and Derbyshire) with the addition of an intrusive -n-.

English: from the Middle English (Old French) female personal name Orenge, probably a variant of Orege (from Latin Oregia) with Anglo-Norman French intrusive -n- before -g-, as in English passenger for Old French passager.

variants.name: Orris
meanings: English (East Anglia): probably in most instances a variant of Orridge (see Orange 2 and 3), with sibilation of the final palatal consonant to /ʃ/ and /s/ to give (H)orrish and (H)orris. It was sometimes associated with the personal name Horace in modern times by folk etymology.

English (Essex): occasionally perhaps from the Middle English personal name Orace (from Latin Horatius, possibly of Etruscan origin) but it was exceptionally rare in medieval England and is unlikely to be the main source of the surname.

variants.name: Horwich
meanings: English (Lancashire): habitational name from Horwich (Lancashire), probably from Old English hār ‘gray, stone’, also ‘boundary’, + wice ‘wych elm’. See also Horace .

nameMeanings.name: Bertrand
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Medieval French variant of   Bertram  , imported to Britain by the Normans. In modern times it has been made famous by the English philosopher  Bertrand Russell  (  1872–1970 ), but is only in occasional use.

nameMeanings.name: Ier
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: from Yiddish ber ‘bear’, probably influenced by the early medieval European practice of giving animal names to people. It is often paired with Dov in order to provide a Hebrew name in certain rituals.

en.lifeSummary: When Bertrand Ier de Baux d'Orange was born in 1137, in Les Baux-de-Provence, Bouches-du-Rhône, France, his father, Raymond Ier des Baux, was 55 and his mother, Étiennette de Gévaudan-Provence, was 39. He had at least 3 sons and 3 daughters with Thiburge de Montpellier. He died on 5 April 1181, in La Roque-d'Anthéron, Bouches-du-Rhône, Provence-Alpes-Côte d'Azur, France, at the age of 44, and was buried in La Roque-d'Anthéron, Bouches-du-Rhône, France.

Il a eu au moins 3 fils et 3 filles avec Thiburge de Montpellier. ja.lifeSummary: Bertrand Ier de Baux d'Orangeが1137年にフランスボウチェス-ドウ-ルhoンレス バウクス-デ-プローベンスで生まれた時，父Raymond Ier des Bauxは55歳，母Étiennette de Gévaudan-Provenceは39歳であった。彼はThiburge de Montpellierとの間に少なくとも3人の息子と3人の娘をもうけた。彼は1181年4月5日にフランスプローベンセ-アルペス-クoト ド'アズルボウチェス-ドウ-ルhoンラ ロクエ-ド'アンスeロンで44歳で死去し，フランスボウチェス-ドウ-ルhoンラ ロクエ-ド'アンスeロンにて埋葬された。

ko.Les Baux-de-Provence, Bouches-du-Rhône,: 프랑스 프로븐스-알페스-co트 드'아저 부체스-드-ho니 레스 바스-데-프로븐스
ko.lifeSummary: Bertrand Ier de Baux d'Orange(이)가 1137년 프랑스 부체스-드-ho니 레스 바스-데-프로븐스에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Raymond Ier des Baux(은)는 55세였고, 그의 어머니 Étiennette de Gévaudan-Provence(은)는 39세였다. 그는 Thiburge de Montpellier(와)과 적어도 세 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1181년 4월 5일 프랑스 프로븐스-알페스-co트 드'아저 부체스-드-ho니 라 로크-드'아스e런에서 44세의 나이로 사망했으며 프랑스 부체스-드-ho니 라 로크-드'아스e런에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Bertrand Ier de Baux d'Orange nasceu em 1137, em Les Baux-de-Provence, Bouches-du-Rhône, França, seu pai, Raymond Ier des Baux, tinha 55 anos e sua mãe, Étiennette de Gévaudan-Provence, tinha 39 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 3 filhos e 3 filhas com Thiburge de Montpellier. Ele faleceu em 5 de abril de 1181, em La Roque-d'Anthéron, Bouches-du-Rhône, Provence-Alpes-Côte d'Azur, França, com 44 anos, e foi sepultado em La Roque-d'Anthéron, Bouches-du-Rhône, França.

ru.La Roque-d'Anthéron, Bouches-du-Rhône, P: ла рокуе-д'антeрон, боучес-ду-рoн, провенсе-алпес-кoт д'азур, Франция

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Bertrand Ier de Baux d'Orange родился в 1137 г. в местности лесь баукс-де-провенс, боучес-ду-рoн, Франция, его отцу по имени Raymond Ier des Baux было 55 лет, а матери, которую звали Étiennette de Gévaudan-Provence, 39. У него было по крайней мере 3 сына и 3 дочери от Thiburge de Montpellier. Он умер 5 апреля 1181 г. в местности ла рокуе-д'антeрон, боучес-ду-рoн, провенсе-алпес-кoт д'азур, Франция в возрасте 44 лет и был похоронен в местности ла рокуе-д'антeрон, боучес-ду-рoн, Франция.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Bertrand Ier de Baux d'Orange nació en 1137, en Les Baux-de-Provence, Bouches-du-Rhône, Francia, su papá, Raymond Ier des Baux, tenía 55 años y su mamá, Étiennette de Gévaudan-Provence, tenía 39 años. Tuvo por lo menos 3 hijos y 3 hijas con Thiburge de Montpellier. Murió el 5 de abril de 1181, en La Roque-d'Anthéron, Bouches-du-Rhône, Provence-Alpes-Côte d'Azur, Francia, a la edad de 44 años, y fue sepultado en La Roque-d'Anthéron, Bouches-du-Rhône, Francia.
